record_identity.title: Shadow API entitlement laundering through account farms and transfer stations

summary: Account farms, credential suppliers, transfer stations, or shadow API routers detach model access from its authorised account, identity, payment source, jurisdiction, model, safety configuration, data controller, and downstream beneficiary, then resell that capacity as fungible inference.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This makes model access a launderable entitlement. It can defeat controls, obscure responsibility, substitute models, alter safety behaviour, capture prompts or secrets, enable unauthorised distillation, and survive enforcement through replaceable account pools. The failure is jurisdiction-neutral and excludes legitimate transparent aggregation.

source_records.relevance_note: Identifies Chinese 中转站 or transfer stations and describes account merchants, rotating accounts, subscription subdivision, possible payment abuse, model substitution, token inflation, and possible log monetisation.

system_context.specific_model_or_runtime: Claude, shadow APIs, transfer stations, commercial proxies, hydra-cluster account pools, and multi-provider routers
system_context.interface_surface: APIs, proxy endpoints, subscriptions, cloud access, routers, coding agents, payment rails, identity workflows, and account pools
system_context.deployment_context: Cross-border routed inference, with current evidence concentrated in Chinese transfer-station markets but structurally applicable wherever credentials are fungible.
system_context.user_role: account holder, API customer, developer, researcher, reseller, proxy operator, credential supplier, provider, or affected third party
system_context.affected_population: legitimate account and payment holders, downstream customers, developers exposing code or secrets, research users, providers, and identity-verification subjects

jurisdictional_context.sector: frontier AI / cloud inference / software / research / identity / payments
jurisdictional_context.jurisdictional_assessment: China is the most visible current evidence base, not part of the canonical definition. The failure depends on broken entitlement, provenance, safety, identity, model, data-controller, or accountability bindings.

failure_mode_definition: An intermediary or distributed supply chain aggregates, rotates, acquires, compromises, subsidises, subdivides, or brokers model-access credentials while obscuring or severing material bindings among the authorised principal, account, identity, payment instrument, entitlement source, provider, jurisdiction, requested and served model, safety configuration, data controller, functional beneficiary, and accountable operator.
failure_threshold: The threshold is met when non-transferable access is resold; accounts, credits, subscriptions, keys, identities, payments, or cloud pathways are farmed, compromised, fraudulently obtained, or rotated; the beneficiary cannot be attributed; controls attach only to a proxy identity; model or safety identity cannot be verified; prompts, outputs, secrets, or tool calls are retained, altered, injected, exfiltrated, or repurposed without authority; or replaceable accounts make enforcement ineffective. Authorised transparent aggregation does not qualify merely because an intermediary exists.

failure_classification.likelihood: active and recurring where demand, pricing differentials, regional restrictions, reusable credentials, opaque intermediaries, and API-compatible interfaces coexist
failure_classification.confidence: corroborated; prevalence and operator intent remain source-specific
failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: Replaceable accounts, credentials, operators, and routes allow services to reconstitute after enforcement.
failure_classification.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — security-integrity / access-entitlement integrity / intermediary provenance; FCS.PROVISIONAL
failure_classification.persistence: active while access can be detached and resold without end-to-end entitlement, model, data-controller, beneficiary, and safety provenance
failure_classification.reproducibility: controlled proxy purchasing, model fingerprinting, billing comparison, canary testing, account-cluster analysis, provider telemetry, and longitudinal monitoring
failure_classification.visibility: discounted API offers, alternate endpoints, bulk-account services, model divergence, inconsistent safety, unexplained usage, rotated keys, malicious injection, and service reconstitution

triage.triage_owner: VIGIL maintainers with CAM SECURITY, OPERATIONS, LATTICE, arbitration, provenance, and economic-legitimacy review
triage.mitigation_status: unresolved — adjacent doctrine exists, but an explicit entitlement-chain and intermediary-accountability primitive has not been confirmed.
triage.recommended_next_step: Audit Caelestis for entitlement integrity, authorised intermediaries, beneficiary and data-controller provenance, model attestation, safety preservation, credential-pool classification, responsibility, and legitimate-aggregation boundaries.

cam_internal.governance_layer: access-entitlement integrity / intermediary assurance / sovereign-boundary porosity / model and safety provenance / supply-chain accountability
cam_internal.proposal_needed: yes — assess Access Entitlement Integrity or Credential-Pooled Inference Laundering after corpus review
routing_note: VIGIL records the ecosystem failure; normative repair belongs in Caelestis.
The failure is jurisdiction-neutral and distinguishes legitimate aggregation from abuse.
No universal telemetry, vendor tethering, nationality-tiered access, capability ablation, or presumed guilt is endorsed.
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: new failure-mode record; generated indexes should rebuild deterministically

repair_status.verification_note: No explicit complete CAM repair has been verified.
remaining_gaps: End-to-end principal, beneficiary, payment, jurisdiction, model, safety, and data-controller binding.

ecosystem_status.basis: 2026 evidence describes an established transfer-station market, provider-observed hydra clusters, shadow API model and safety divergence, and malicious router behaviour. Replaceable accounts and routes prevent individual suspension from resolving the supply chain.

corpus_coverage.coverage_summary: Account-resource, access-state, Sovereign Assurance Boundary, provenance, security, and functional-contribution doctrine appears adjacent; complete coverage is not asserted.
remaining_gaps: Beneficiary, model, safety, payment, jurisdiction, and data-controller provenance.
Proportionate responses preserving legitimate decentralised pathways.

review_history.review_outcome: New recurring security-integrity failure mode warranted, with geography, provider allegations, prevalence, and legitimate aggregation expressly qualified.
